content.claim: Structural teacher shortage in Hungary is characterized by low relative pay (among lowest in Europe), high student-teacher ratios, and requirement for teachers to perform non-teaching duties, creating thin workforce capacity for major system reforms requiring widespread buy-in and implementation fidelity.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Teacher shortage is documented as structural fact, but specific implementation consequence for a track-reform scenario is inference rather than direct evidence
No quantitative labor-supply elasticity study provided to estimate whether capacity shortage is permanent or would resolve under different conditions (e.g., pay increases)
International comparison of teacher conditions does not establish Hungary-specific threshold below which system reforms become undeliverable
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
